strategy: Balance boss; she opens Round 1 with a -75% Doom and Gloom that cuts healing. Clear it with Strangle rather than a Global, which she just recasts.
Do not cast blades. A blade makes her interrupt with her natural attack, Steal Charm, and a Weakness on the caster; Aegis saves the blade but not the weakness.
Set up with traps instead of blades; traps do not trigger her, and blades given by spells or Shadow Shrike's Dragonspear are also safe.
Feints are risky too; a Feint gives a chance of Virulent Plague, and Life Feint also triggers it.
Every fifth Round she casts Sand Wurm at a random wizard; keep a heal ready on that timer.
Trap up, Feint sparingly, and finish with one hit.
cheatNotes: Joining late gets you Mana Burned; enter on time.
She opens with a -75% Doom and Gloom that cripples healing; clear it with Strangle rather than a Global.
If you overwrite the Doom and Gloom with another Global, she just recasts it; use Strangle instead.
Strangle bypasses the Doom and Gloom cheat cleanly.
If she does keep a replacement Global, it stays put; still, Strangle is the reliable answer.
Every fifth Round she hits a random wizard with Sand Wurm; keep a heal ready on that timer.
Casting a blade makes her interrupt with a hit, Steal Charm, and a Weakness on you; set up with traps instead.
A Global like Darkwind also triggers that punish.
On a multi-target blade she only hits the caster.
Shadow Shrike's Dragonspear does not trigger the blade punish.
Aegis protects the blade from the steal, but she still lands the weakness.
Blades handed to you by spells do not trigger the punish.
Pet-cast blades do trigger it, so watch your pet.
Feinting her risks a Virulent Plague; Feint sparingly.
Life Feint also risks the Plague.
